business.headline: Why RBI drew the line on mis-selling by banksThe RBI has issued its strongest framework yet against mis-selling by banks and regulated entities. The regulations target dark patterns, forced bundling and unsuitable sales while shifting accountability to institutions.

Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningAccenture reported strong AI demand but trimmed its growth outlook, signalling that clients are still holding back on spending. The warning hammered Indian IT stocks, with Infosys leading the rout.

Jio IPO moves ahead as Reliance bets big on AI and new energyRIL AGM 2026: Jio Platforms to file IPO papers with Sebi; Mukesh Ambani unveils five major value creation pathways, bets big on AI, energy self-reliance, satellite connectivity, media, FMCG and global expansion

Sensex down 700 points: Why is stock market falling today after a 5-day rally?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 635.85 points, or 0.84%, at 76,774.13, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 170.10 points, or 0.70%, to 23,997.90 as of 10:13 am. The decline snapped a strong rally that had seen the Sensex gain nearly 4.8% and the Nifty rise over 4.3% in the previous five sessions.

Reliance AGM: Will Mukesh Ambani finally reveal the Jio IPO date today?Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to address shareholders at the company's 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM) today, and the biggest question on Dalal Street is simple: Will he finally announce a timeline for the Jio IPO?

Accenture warning sends Infosys, TCS and HCLTech tumbling. Here's whyMajor Indian IT stocks fell sharply after Accenture lowered the upper end of its annual revenue growth forecast. The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Accenture warning triggers IT crash on Dalal StreetInfosys, TCS and other technology stocks tumbled after the global IT giant flagged slower growth, dragging the broader market lower despite falling oil prices.

India's Swiss bank funds fall 8%, while direct deposits jump 50%Swiss National Bank data showed total funds linked to Indian clients fell in 2025 even as direct customer deposits rose sharply. The figures underline that Indian-linked assets in Switzerland are not the same as black money estimates and reflect a more transparent banking regime.

Jio IPO, AI, green energy: 5 big questions Mukesh Ambani may answer at RIL AGMMukesh Ambani is set to face shareholder questions on Jio's IPO timeline, AI plans and green energy projects at Reliance's 49th AGM. With key deadlines nearing, investors are seeking proof that earlier commitments are moving into execution.

Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

NSE IPO filing could unlock Rs 25,000 crore for SBI, Temasek and top investorsNSE has filed draft papers for its long-delayed initial public offering after nearly a decade. The move could unlock major gains for existing investors and value the exchange at about $57 billion.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have sought a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission considers revisions to salaries and pensions. The final multiplier will determine how much basic pay and pensions rise for millions of central government employees and retirees.

Sensex closes 254 points higher, Nifty near 24,200; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 254.36 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,409.98, while the NSE Nifty50 gained around 82 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 24,168.

Crude oil is no longer the villain. A new threat could raise your household billEconomists say a weak monsoon has overtaken crude oil as India's main inflation risk. Poor rainfall could raise food prices, squeeze household budgets and complicate RBI rate cuts.

EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

entertainment.headline: Nayanthara cheers for Samantha as Maa Inti Bangaaram earns Rs 25 crore worldwide Actor Nayanthara congratulated actor Samantha Ruth Prabhu after Maa Inti Bangaaram scored a strong opening at the box office. The film has crossed Rs 25 crore mark globally.

AR Rahman shares teaser of final Asha Bhosle recording on World Music Day. Watch Composer AR Rahman released a teaser of a special tribute song featuring the late Asha Bhosle on World Music Day. Recorded before her death in April 2026, the track is among the final recordings of the legendary singer's illustrious career.

Shwetha Menon, first woman president, announces resignation from film body The AMMA executive committee led by actor Shwetha Menon resigned after a no-confidence motion at its annual meeting. The abrupt exit ended a historic leadership and sharpened questions over accounts and administration.

Actor Gracy Singh celebrates 25 years of Lagaan, remembers her iconic role as Gauri As Lagaan completed 25 years, Gracy Singh reflected on the film's lasting impact and shared that her character Gauri became her second name among fans.

Javed Akhtar hits back at troll over Islamophobic comment, calls himself an atheist Veteran lyricist Javed Akhtar hit back at a troll over an Islamophobic comment. The user had responded to Akhtar's social media post criticising Donald Trump's remarks on Giorgia Meloni.

Director James Burrows death: Jennifer Aniston, Courtney Cox recall Friends days Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox paid emotional tributes to legendary television director James Burrows following his death at 85. The Friends stars remembered the Emmy-winning filmmaker as a mentor and father figure who helped shape their careers and the iconic sitcom.

Carry on Jatta 4 trailer: Film packs laughs, nostalgia, tribute to Jaswinder Bhalla The trailer of Carry On Jatta 4 reunites the franchise's comic ensemble and key throwbacks. Its VFX recreation of late actor Jaswinder Bhalla and the return of the Ghodi sharpen the nostalgia ahead of the 26 June release.

Samay Raina slams sexist remark on Latent 2, fans praise him for taking a stand A clip from India's Got Latent shows Samay Raina flipping a sexist audience remark back on the speaker. The moment has gone viral with fans praising the comedian for taking a stand.

Taylor Swift extends wedding invite to Blake Lively after rift? Here's what we know Taylor Swift has reportedly extended a wedding invitation to Blake Lively as the longtime friends work towards repairing their relationship after a fallout.

From Myositis battle to motherhood: Samantha's journey of strength at 39 Amid Samantha's pregnancy at 39, the spotlight has also returned to the actor's inspiring journey of battling myositis and rebuilding her health after years of complications.

Imtiaz Ali 'hairaan' as Main Vaapas Aaunga doubles box office on 2nd Friday Imtiaz Ali said he was surprised and overwhelmed after Main Vaapas Aaunga nearly doubled its opening Friday collections on its second Friday. The filmmaker credited strong word of mouth and audience support for the film's unexpected box-office turnaround.

Toxic new release date: Yash film to hit theatres in August after multiple delays Actor Yash has announced a new release date for Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups after the film faced multiple postponements. It now slated to release in August.

Amitabh Bachchan, Ranbir Kapoor attend Supriya Sule's daughter Revati's wedding Amitabh Bachchan, Jaya Bachchan, Shweta Bachchan, Ranbir Kapoor and several Bollywood celebrities attended the wedding of Supriya Sule's daughter Revati and Sarang Lakhani in Mumbai.

Is Anurag Kashyap a Bollywood nationalist now? Bollywood's biggest rebel has a new complaint: not enough screens. But when did the man who fought entitlement start sounding like he is owed one? Here's more on the curious case of Bandar and Anurag Kashyap's most twisty argument yet.

Maa Inti Bangaaram box office Day 2: Samantha film races past Rs 25 crore globally Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram posed a sharp rise in collections on its second day. The jump, driven mainly by the Telugu version and steady overseas business, has strengthened its opening weekend run.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 2: Shahid, Kriti film crosses Rs 50-crore mark globally Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna's Cocktail 2 added Rs 16.25 crore on Saturday, taking its two-day India nett total to Rs 29.75 crore. The sequel also crossed Rs 50.95 crore worldwide.

Lowest point of her career: Samay Raina's brutal roast of Alia Bhatt on Latent 2 Samay Raina opened India's Got Latent Season 2 by roasting Alia Bhatt and Sharvari during their Alpha promotions. From taking digs at Alia's Cannes appearance and Jigra, the actor was not spared from Samay's trademark no-holds-barred humour.

India's Got Latent 2 X review: Upset fans ask Samay Raina, 'Where's the old magic?' Samay Raina's India's Got Latent Season 2 has received mixed reactions after its premiere with Alia Bhatt and Sharvari. While some viewers enjoyed parts of the episode, many felt the show had lost the raw, unfiltered humour that made the first season a hit.

Shraddha Kapoor's brother can't hold back tears after Eetha teaser: My jaan sister Actor Shraddha Kapoor's brother Siddhanth shared an emotional reaction after watching the teaser of her upcoming film Eetha. His message came as the first glimpse of the film drew praise online for Shraddha's transformation.

general news.headline: We made great progress, now we see future together: Vance after talks with Iran Live updatesSpeaking after negotiations with Iranian officials at the Swiss resort of Burgenstock, JD Vance said the discussions had yielded advances on several fronts and reflected President Donald Trump's push for breakthrough in the Middle East.

Trump warns Iran to stop proxies in Lebanon or US will hit Tehran 'very hard again'The warning landed at a delicate moment for the peace process. The US and Iranian deligation are currently meeting in Switzerland under a memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed earlier this month.

Remarks against us changed our minds: Rebel UBT MP confirms switch to Shinde SenaShiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashtikar confirmed his switch to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, citing a lack of development funds and the limitations of being in the opposition. He said he had not abandoned his ideology, calling the move a shift from "one Shiv Sena to another," while rejecting claims of personal differences with Uddhav Thackeray.

PM Modi waited at Delhi airport for 45 minutes. It was linked to NEETPrime Minister Narendra Modi stayed back at Delhi Airport on Sunday as students travelled for the NEET-UG re-examination. The move was aimed at keeping traffic smooth and avoiding any disruption to candidates heading to their centres.

NEET re-test tougher than before? Students flag difficult physics, easier biology  Live UpdatesMany of the students across India who appeared for the NEET-UG re-test described physics as tougher and lengthier than before. Biology was largely seen as easy and NCERT-based, while chemistry remained moderate. Feedback from Delhi, Chandigarh, Chennai, Mumbai, Pune, Bhopal and Srinagar revealed varied reactions.

I have an Indian, Pakistani in my life: Vance's joke after Iran talks goes viralUS Vice President JD Vance sparked buzz online with a light-hearted remark, naming his wife, Usha Vance, and Pakistani Army chief Asim Munir as the two most "favourite" people in his life after the US-Iran talks in Switzerland.

Crackdown in Gurugram's DLF Phase-3 for violations, many left homeless overnightThe Town and Country Planning Department (DTCP) has intensified demolition and sealing action in Gurugram's DLF Phase-3 over illegal commercial use of residential buildings. The High Court-backed drive has expanded after a large survey found widespread violations and infrastructure strain.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revenge-arc destroys Sri Lanka in tri-series finalIndia A routed Sri Lanka A in the final of the tri-nation series. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yash Thakur starred with the bat and ball respectively as India hammered Sahan Arachchige's side in Dambulla.

Cancer served on a plate? How chemicals banned in Europe are killing IndiansCarcinogenic chemicals, which get Indian agricultural exports rejected in Europe, continue to be sprayed at Indian farms without checks even though they are banned abroad. Why does the government allow these chemicals to find a place on Indian farms and then reach plates?

IND vs SA Live Score: Jemimah, Harman key as Yastika departsIndia (IND-W) vs South Africa (SA-W) Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Ayobonga Khaka has taken the big wicket of Yastika Bhatia as India have lost momentum they gained in the powerplay. Harmanpreet Kaur has joined Jemimah Rodrigues in the middle.

7 killed, 67 critical in Tamil Nadu ammonia leak; CM Vijay orders probeAn ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing factory in Tamil Nadu's Tiruvallur affected at least 67 people on Saturday. Six workers are in very critical condition as authorities investigate the cause and emergency teams contain the site.

Mumbai rain: How monsoon showers finally arrived after being held hostageMumbai woke up on June 21 to passing showers across its suburbs, breaking weeks of an unusual dry spell that had pushed the city's reservoirs to critically low levels. The IMD has issued a yellow alert for June 22 and 23, with heavier rainfall expected around June 25 and 26 as the monsoon strengthens further.

Caught on CCTV: Gujarat teen crashes car into scooter, throws rider into airA 17-year-old boy, returning from a yoga session, crashed his car into a scooter in Gujarat's Rajkot, critically injuring the scooter rider. The entire incident was captured on CCTV. Police said an FIR will be filed against the teenager's father.

He left the IIT path. Today, 1.5 crore people in 169 countries do yoga with himSaurabh Bothra, an IIT-BHU graduate, built Habuild into a global online yoga platform reaching 15 million learners in 169 countries. The journey from personal practice to daily digital sessions shows how consistency turned yoga into a habit for millions.

From Myositis battle to motherhood: Samantha's journey of strength at 39Amid Samantha's pregnancy at 39, the spotlight has also returned to the actor's inspiring journey of battling myositis and rebuilding her health after years of complications.

Live: NEET re-exam ends, students find Physics lengthy, Chemistry moderateNEET UG Re-Exam Live Updates: More than 22 lakh candidates are appearing for the NEET UG 2026 re-exam being held from 2 pm to 5:15 pm across 5,440 centres in 551 cities. The test is underway under strict security arrangements, including biometric verification, frisking, CCTV surveillance and enhanced monitoring at exam centres.

Rare tornado spins over Thoothukudi, a sight Tamil Nadu almost never sees. WatchA rare tornado touched down over Thoothukudi in Tamil Nadu on June 21, a state that almost never sees one. Here is what a tornado is, how it forms and why these twisters stay so unusual in southern India.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

How coaching giants are building mini-IITs in Kota and SikarThey are not IITs, nor do they claim to be one. Yet the coaching institutes of Rajasthan's Kota and Sikar, which prepare lakhs of engineering and medical aspirants, have grown into mini-cities whose scale, influence, and infrastructure are comparable to some IITs.

Smashed windows, bloodied face: 2 BBA students arrested for Gurugram road rageA video of the incident has since gone viral on social media, where the survivor was seen urging commuters to stop the two students. His face was seen as badly injured.

Is Anurag Kashyap a Bollywood nationalist now?Bollywood's biggest rebel has a new complaint: not enough screens. But when did the man who fought entitlement start sounding like he is owed one? Here's more on the curious case of Bandar and Anurag Kashyap's most twisty argument yet.

NEET aspirant with 9 broken ribs takes re-test with Dharmendra Pradhan's supportA week after suffering severe injuries in a road accident, NEET aspirant Shrishti Dubey appeared for the re-examination with extensive medical support. Following intervention by Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, authorities arranged a separate room, doctors, paramedics and an ambulance at her Kolkata centre.

Andhra woman and lover kill ailing husband, pass off murder as heart attackA woman and her lover in Andhra Pradesh's Kakinada district were arrested on charges of killing her heart patient husband after they mixed his juice with sleeping pills and suffocated him to death. The accused initially passed off the murder as a heart attack.

The Great IIT Reversal: Why are toppers choosing Civil Engineering over AI?Handcrafted ByOur Editors For years, the IIT dream meant Computer Science. But as AI reshapes careers and India's infrastructure boom gathers pace, some of the country's top JEE Advanced rankers are taking a fresh look at Civil Engineering. Their choices may indicate changing aspirations among India's future engineers.

sports.headline: West Indies edge closer to semis after bowlers run riot against Sri Lanka Women's T20 World Cup, SL vs WI: West Indies have continued their impressive run in the tournament, beating Sri Lanka by five wickets in Bristol and strengthening their chances of qualifying for the semis.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revenge-arc destroys Sri Lanka in tri-series final India A routed Sri Lanka A in the final of the tri-nation series. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yash Thakur starred with the bat and ball respectively as India hammered Sahan Arachchige's side in Dambulla.

IND vs SA Live Score: Yastika departs as India lose momentum India (IND-W) vs South Africa (SA-W) Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Marizanne Kapp has taken the big wicket of Smriti Mandhana, who was looking good for a big score. Yastika Bhatia has joined Yastika Bhatia in the middle.

Wahab Riaz blames Pakistan's own mistakes for exit: We beat ourselves Women's T20 World Cup, PAK vs BAN: Pakistan coach Wahab Riaz blamed his team's repeated batting collapses for their early exit from the tournament, saying Pakistan had "beaten themselves" rather than being outplayed by opponents.

Where is Mohammed Siraj? Internet abuzz after England ODI squad omission India left Mohammed Siraj out of the ODI squad for England after resting him from the T20Is. With no fresh explanation from the BCCI, the omission has quickly opened questions over workload management, form and selection priorities.

Premier League Buzz: What's happening in the off season? World Cup performances and boardroom churn are accelerating transfer and managerial decisions across the Premier League. From Bouaddi's surge to Rashford's uncertain future, clubs are reshaping squads under pressure.

Ben Stokes to captain England again? Durham star pulled from County clash The ECB withdrew Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson from their County Championship matches on Sunday, signalling a likely England return for the third Test. The move points to a one-Test suspension over the curfew breach as the enquiry continues.

No place for Yashasvi Jaiswal in ODI squad, Varun Chakravarthy ruled of Ireland T20Is India left out Yashasvi Jaiswal for the England ODIs and lost Varun Chakravarthy for the Ireland T20Is. The calls expose fierce competition for places and a squad already being tested by injury.

IND vs ENG: Virat Kohli's selection subject to fitness as BCCI announces ODI squad India tour of England: Former captain Virat Kohli has been included in the 15-man squad, subject to him clearing a fitness test. Kohli missed the recently-concluded series against Afghanistan due to an injury sustained during the IPL 2026 final.

Eloy Room demands a statue in Curacao after making Tim Howard sweat, Ecuador weepFIFA World Cup: Eloy Room inspired Curacao to a 0-0 draw against Ecuador with 15 saves in Kansas City. The result gave the debutants their first World Cup point while the goalkeeper missed an all-time record by a save.

First Asian team to score four at World Cup as Japan beat Tunisia on historic nightFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan thrashed Tunisia 4-0 in the 1,000th FIFA World Cup match to move close to qualification. The win underlined Japan's control and sent Tunisia out after a second straight loss.

A World Cup a year: How cricket lost the soul football managed to saveOnce a sacred pilgrimage, cricket's endless cycle of annual global tournaments has commodified anticipation, trading the heartbreaking magic of long-term yearning for short-term broadcast revenue, which is in stark contrast to football's fiercely guarded quadrennial scarcity.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hits fastest List A fifty in vengeful 94 vs Sri LankaTri-series final: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hammered an 11-ball fifty and finished with 94 off 29 balls against Sri Lanka A on Sunday. The innings rewrote the List A record books and answered the week's flashpoint in Dambulla.

Tri-series final Highlights: Vaibhav Sooryanshi storms sinks hosts in finalIndia (IND A) vs Sri Lanka (SL A) Tri-Series Final Highlights: India won the tri-nation series after beating Sri Lanka by 66 runs. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi laid the platform with a 94-run knock, after which the visitors bowled the hosts out for 311 in 47.1 overs.

Curacao had no room. Then came Eloy Room. Meet their FIFA World Cup heroFIFA World Cup 2026: Eloy Room made 15 saves as Curaçao held Ecuador 0-0 at the World Cup. The draw delivered the nation its first point and turned the 37-year-old into a global phenomenon.

Neeraj Chopra not surprised by Sri Lankan Rumesh Pathirage's rapid riseNeeraj Chopra said Rumesh Pathirage's breakthrough did not surprise him after their meetings last year. The Sri Lankan's surge, which includes a record 92.62m throw, has intensified South Asian javelin competition before the upcoming Commonwealth Games.

Superman Eloy Room inspires Curacao to historic first World Cup point vs EcuadorEloy Room made 15 saves as Curacao held Ecuador to a goalless draw at the World Cup. The result delivered the island nation its first point and kept slim knockout hopes alive.

FIFA World Cup: Brazil to keep faith in Raphinha despite latest injury setbackRaphinha went off with a right thigh muscle injury during Brazil's 3-0 win over Haiti. Brazil want him to stay with the squad and return for the tournament later on.

India face stiff South Africa test in Harmanpreet Kaur's historic outingWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: Harmanpreet Kaur's India and Laura Wolvaardt's South Africa will face off in a crucial Group A clash on Sunday in Manchester with the race to the semifinals heating up.

USA defends Iran's World Cup 2026 travel restrictions despite outburst from teamThe United States has defended Iran's World Cup travel regime, even as the team threatens to file a FIFA complaint. Iran has been forced to forcefully leave the country early after their matches during the tournament.

England maintain perfect start with 38-run win vs sloppy ScotlandEngland beat Scotland by 38 runs after posting another 200-plus total. Sophia Dunkley punished three reprieves from Scotland and Sophie Ecclestone wrapped upa fine day with her 150th T20I wicket.

Fire and ice: Temper trial awaits Tilak Varma's India A in tri-series finalIndia A are playing Sri Lanka A in the tri-series final. After two absolute thrillers between Tilak Varma and Sahan Arachchige's sides in the round-robin phase, will the tempers flare once again?

TUN 0 vs 4 JPN Highlights: Japan thrash Tunisia on historic World Cup nightTunisia (TUN) vs Japan (JPN) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (TUN 0 - 4 JPN) Japan put four past Tunisia to stamp to go second in Group F. Tunisia becomes third nation to crash out of the World Cup 2026 after Haiti and Turkiye. Earlier, Curacao had created history. They held Ecuador to a 0-0 draw. A sensational show from the smallest nation in the tournament.

Super sub Deniz Undav becomes hero as Germany beat Ivory Coast 2-1 in thrillerDeniz Undav came off the bench to score twice as Germany beat Ivory Coast 2-1 in Toronto. Julian Nagelsmann's changes proved decisive and pushed Germany into the next round of the competition.

ENG vs NZ: Joe Root creates history, but New Zealand on course for Oval Test winJoe Root reached 14,000 Test runs as England slipped deep into a daunting chase against New Zealand at The Oval. His unbeaten 75 kept the hosts alive, but the visitors need only five wickets to level the series.

Beyond the game: The three women making history at the FIFA World Cup 2026Tori Penso, Brooke Mayo and Kathryn Nesbitt are breaking barriers at FIFA World Cup 2026 as the tournament's only all-women officiating crew.

Brazil President mocks Neymar's World Cup absence with 'Work-From-Home' jibeFIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil President Lula joked that Neymar is the national team's first "work-from-home" player as the injured star continues his recovery and pushes to return for Brazil's World Cup match against Scotland.

Ronald Koeman's Dutch answer critics in style with five-star rout of SwedenNED vs SWE, FIFA World Cup 2026: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo scored twice each as the Netherlands thrashed Sweden 5-1 in Houston. Crysencio Summerville added a late fifth as Ronald Koeman's side climbed atop Group F.

India will miss Shreyanka Patil, but Prema Rawat is equally exciting: India bowling coachWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: India bowling coach Aavishkar Salvi said that India will miss Shreyanka Patil, who has been ruled out due to a foot injury, but he hailed young leg-spinner Prema Rawat as an equally exciting prospect.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
